title: Fraxinus parryi
taxonomy: Fraxinus parryi is a species of ash tree that belongs to the family Oleaceae. It is a deciduous tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The genus Fraxinus is a group of trees commonly known as ash trees, and Fraxinus parryi is one of the 46 species within this genus. The classification of Fraxinus parryi is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, the division Magnoliophyta, and the class Magnoliopsida. Its specific classification is Fraxinus parryi, a member of the order Lamiales.
uses: Fraxinus parryi, also known as Parry's ash, has been traditionally used in folk medicine by Native American communities. The plant's bark, leaves, and roots are reported to have medicinal properties. Fraxinus parryi is said to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which may help in treating various health issues. The plant's extracts have been used to treat fever, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. However, more research is needed to fully understand the potential medicinal uses and benefits of Fraxinus parryi.
benefits: Fraxinus parryi, also known as the Colorado blue spruce's relative, is not a widely studied species, but Fraxinus parryi, like other ash trees, may offer some health benefits due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. However, more research is needed to confirm these benefits. The tree's leaves, bark, and roots may contain compounds that have been linked to various health benefits in traditional medicine. properties:
Fraxinus parryi is known for its potential therapeutic properties. 
Its extracts have been studied for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which may contribute to its use in traditional medicine.
The plant's bark and leaves have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.

constituents: Fraxinus parryi is a deciduous tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. parts: Fraxinus parryi, commonly known as the California ash, has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The bark of the plant is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The roots of the California ash are also used to create a medicinal tea to treat stomach issues and diarrhea. Additionally, the plant's leaves are used in herbal remedies to treat conditions such as inflammation and wounds. The resin of the tree is also used to create a medicinal ointment to treat skin irritations.
preparations: Fraxinus parryi, also known as Parry's Ash, is a plant with a rich history of traditional use in herbal medicine. The bark of the tree has been used to make a medicinal tea, which is said to have anti-inflammatory properties. In some cultures, the leaves of Fraxinus parryi are used to make a medicinal decoction, which is believed to have antiseptic and antiviral properties.
